title: Data Privacy Associate Director - NRR 4041
Pharmacovigilance Analyst
description:
**Job Summary**


Join our dynamic team as a Pharmacovigilance Specialist where you will play a crucial role in ensuring the safety and efficacy of pharmaceutical products. With a focus on Pharma Research \& Development you will contribute to the monitoring and evaluation of drug safety data. This hybrid role offers a balanced work environment with no travel required allowing you to make a significant impact from the comfort of your preferred location.

**Responsibilities**

* Monitor and evaluate adverse event reports to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.
* Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to analyze and interpret safety data.
* Assist in the preparation of safety reports and regulatory submissions.
* Support the development and maintenance of pharmacovigilance systems and processes.
* Conduct literature reviews to identify potential safety signals.
* Provide input into risk management plans and safety assessments.
* Ensure timely and accurate case processing and data entry.
* Participate in safety review meetings and contribute to decision\-making processes.
* Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge of pharmacovigilance regulations and guidelines.
* Assist in the training and mentoring of junior team members.
* Contribute to the continuous improvement of pharmacovigilance practices.
* Engage with stakeholders to communicate safety findings and recommendations.
* Utilize technical skills in Pharma Research \& Development to enhance data analysis capabilities.

**Qualifications**

* Possess a degree in a relevant scientific discipline.
* Demonstrate experience in Pharma Research \& Development.
* Exhibit strong analytical and problem\-solving skills.
* Show proficiency in pharmacovigilance data management.
* Have experience in PV Case Processing as a nice to have skill.
* Display excellent communication and teamwork abilities.
* Maintain a keen attention to detail and accuracy.

**Certifications Required**


Certification in Pharmacovigilance or Drug Safety Management.

title: Analista de Riesgos de IT
description:
**BIND** es un ecosistema integrado de servicios bancarios y financieros líder en innovación en Argentina, compuesto por BIND Banco, BIND Garantías, BIND Inversiones, BIND Seguros, BIND Broker de Seguros, BIND PSP, BIND Leasing, bindX, Credicuotas, Tienda Júbilo, Poincenot Tech Studio y NatanVC.


Buscamos transformar la manera en que los servicios financieros llegan a las empresas y personas.


Ofrecemos soluciones ágiles y simples que empoderan e impactan a millones de usuarios.


En esta ocasión, **estamos buscando sumar un rol de Analista de Riesgos de TI**

**El desafío:**

* Análisis y gestión de riesgos tecnológicos
	+ Inventario de activos tecnológicos: Asegurar la integridad del inventario. Conocer los principales activos del Bind incluyendo las empresas del grupo y su funcionamiento.
	+ Armar el cronograma anual de Autoevaluaciones de Riesgos tecnológicos, seguridad de la información y proveedores de la Entidad y Empresas Vinculadas, seguimiento y control.
	+ Capacitar a los responsables/referentes de las distintas áreas y empresas del grupo en lo referente a la identificación de los riesgos tecnológicos.
	+ Confeccionar informes de gestión, identificando los principales riesgos y debilidades de cada activo.
	+ Confeccionar Informes de riesgos Tecnológicos de nuevos productos, procesos, negocios y aplicativos, identificando los principales riesgos asociados y propuesta de controles/mitigantes.
	+ Acompañar a las Gerencias a identificar y mitigar los principales riesgos
	+ Seguimiento de los planes de acción acordados para mitigar los riesgos definidos como críticos.
	+ Revisión y actualización de los procedimientos e instructivos relacionados.
	+ Indicadores de RTI: Cálculo mensual de los indicadores de RTI. En caso de desvíos gestionar las causas /justificación de los mismos con los responsables y propuesta de recalibración de límites y/o umbrales.
	+ Mejora continua de procesos y de la herramienta de gestión (Aleph Manager; Jira)
	+ Armado de información requerida para presentar en el Comité
	+ Propuesta de Automatización de procesos en los que esté involucrado.
	+ Incidentes de TI: Realizar el análisis y seguimiento. En caso de corresponder, gestionar plan de mitigación del riesgo asociado al mismo.
	+ Revisión y propuesta de mejora , de corresponder, de las matrices de riesgos utilizadas.
	+ Proponer mejoras sobre el catálogo de riesgos y controles asociados a la gestión de riesgos tecnológicos.
	+ Aplicabilidad de normativas BCRA (7777, 7783, etc) / ISO / NIST y estándares internacionales (COBIT)

**¿Qué necesitas?**

* Mínimo 3 años como analistas de riesgos de TI o Auditor de Riesgos de TI en Entidades financieras o ConsultorasEstudio Universitario: recibido de Ingeniería de sistemas, analista de sistemas o carreras afines.
* MS Office: intermedio/avanzado.
* Conocimientos de Gestión de Riesgos/Auditoria/Compliance de TI de Entidades financieras o consultoras.
* Experiencia en análisis de riesgos de TI.
* Normativa BCRA (COM “A” 7777 y 7783”); COBIT
* Se valorará conocimientos de la herramienta Jira y Aleph manager

**B****uscamos un perfil:**

* Iniciativa y actitud proactiva
* Visión analítica
* Orientado a resultados y a la mejora de procesos
* Capacidad para interactuar y relacionarse con distintos interlocutores.

title: Student Marketeer, CABA
description:
Nuestros Red Bull Student Marketeers son parte del programa de embajadores de marca y producto más empoderado y dinámico del mundo. Ellos entienden a nuestro target y son responsables de manejar la imagen de la marca y el entendimiento del producto en sus universidades y área de influencia. El objetivo del Student Marketeer Program es alcanzar nuevos consumidores, conectarlos con la marca, aumentar las ventas y promover la marca en sus universidades. Esto incluye el contacto directo con consumidores y clientes, invitándolos a probar el producto, además de trabajar en eventos de Red Bull para asegurar que los consumidores vivan una experiencia inolvidable con la marca.
   

  

**Todas las responsabilidades que te confiaremos:**
**SE UN EMBAJADOR DE LA MARCA Y PRODUCTO**
 Vive y respira la personalidad de la marca y los valores de la empresa, representándola de manera excepcional a través de interacciones diarias con los consumidores y/o clientes. Aumenta la base de consumidores en los lugares y momentos adecuados, respondiendo de manera acertada a cualquiera de sus preguntas. Crea y ejecuta un plan personalizado e innovador en el campus, involucrando a los estudiantes y dando vida a la marca en tu universidad. Establece una sólida red de contactos, construyendo relaciones con personas clave. Comparte contenido unico de la marca a traves de tus redes sociales. Trabaja en eventos de Red Bull y eventos patrocinados para asegurar una experiencia de marca inolvidable para los consumidores.
   

  

**SE UN EXPERTO EN VENTAS**
 Construye relaciones con los gerentes locales de puntos de venta y asegúrate de que Red Bull esté disponible y visible para los consumidores. Colabora con el equipo de ventas para abrir nuevos puntos de distribución de Red Bull en tu campus y alrededores. Aprovecha los recursos relevantes del mundo Red Bull para mantener la marca siempre presente en los puntos de venta de tu area de influencia. Asegura una ejecución perfecta de acuerdo con los estándares definidos para cada punto de venta.
   

  

**EXCELENCIA EN EJECUCION**
 Demuestra excelencia en la ejecución y atención a los detalles en todas las áreas de tu rol. Comparte ideas y colabora con el equipo. Planea y reporta tus actividades a través de la plataforma definida, de manera puntual y precisa. Contribuye al desarrollo de contenidos unicos para las plataformas de Red Bull proporcionando información local, tendencias y oportunidades interesantes.
   

  

**Tus áreas de conocimiento y experiencia que más importan para este rol:**
* Apasionado(a) por la marca y el producto de Red Bull.
* Excelentes habilidades de comunicación.
* Personalidad carismática, enérgica, positiva y extrovertida.
* Inmerso(a) en la vida estudiantil, con amplio conocimiento del entorno universitario, la ciudad y la región, incluyendo actividades extracurriculares y lugares de interés.
* Muy accesible y sociable, con capacidad para relacionarse con diferentes personas en diversas ocasiones.
* Mentalidad creativa y de espíritu emprendedor, con un alto sentido de responsabilidad e iniciativa.
* Capacidad para conciliar el trabajo a tiempo parcial y los estudios.
* Afinidad y deseo de aprender y aplicar nuevas habilidades, especialmente en marketing y ventas.
* Conectado(a) con diversos grupos e individuos influyentes en el campus.


 Ser estudiante universitario en curso
   

  

 Licencia de conducir vigente

title: Analyst, Content Analytics
description:
**BUILD SOMETHING BIGGER THAN YOURSELF**
 The Walt Disney Company, together with its subsidiaries and affiliates, is a leading diversified international family entertainment and media enterprise with the following business segments: Disney Entertainment, ESPN, Disney Parks, and Experiences and Products. From humble beginnings as a cartoon studio in the 1920s to its preeminent name in the entertainment industry today, Disney proudly continues its legacy of creating world\-class stories and experiences for every member of the family. Disney’s stories, characters and experiences reach consumers and guests from every corner of the globe. With operations in more than 40 countries, our employees and cast members work together to create entertainment experiences that are both universally and locally cherished.
   

  

**How will you contribute to our magical experience?**
 The Analyst, Content Analytics will play a pivotal role in transforming raw data into strategic insights that will shape the future of our content and audience experience on Disney Streaming platforms.
   

  

 This role will not just report on performance, but will proactively dive deep into consumption patterns, apply statistical and Machine Learning models to predict trends, and partner directly with stakeholders to influence content at a regional level, uncovering the hidden stories in our data and translate them into actionable recommendations that drive engagement, retention, and business growth.
   

  

**The Analyst, Content Analytics will be responsible for:**
* Create and maintain automated dashboards and reports in BI tools (like Looker) that empower stakeholders to monitor KPIs and explore data independently.
* Work with massive datasets using SQL to extract, transform, and analyze information from various sources. Propose improvements to data ingestion and structure to enable more efficient and robust analyses.
* Design and execute complex analyses to deeply understand the content lifecycle, performance, acquisition and engagement. Apply statistical and Machine Learning techniques to develop models that anticipate consumer behavior and content performance.
* Design and analyze the results of tests and other experiments to validate hypotheses about content and platform features. Stay current on the latest trends in data analytics, Machine Learning, and the entertainment industry.
* Collaborate closely with Content, Marketing and Finance teams to identify key challenges and growth opportunities. Translate business questions into concrete data hypotheses and analyses, presenting results in a clear and compelling manner.


**What we need from you to be part of this story:**
* \+2 years of experience in data analysis, data science, or business intelligence roles, ideally in media, entertainment or tech industries.
* Bachelor’s degree in quantitative fields like Economics, Actuarial Science, Engineering, Statistics, or Computer Science, ideally with a postgraduate degree or specialized courses in Data Science, Business Analytics, or Machine Learning.
* Demonstrable and solid experience using data visualization tools like Looker, Tableau, or Power BI to create interactive dashboards and reports. Proficiency in the Microsoft Office / Google Workspace suite, especially Excel / Google Sheets for ad\-hoc analysis.
* Mastery of analytics and BI tools.
* Advanced, proven experience with SQL for querying and manipulating large datasets in environments like Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, etc.
* Programming experience with Python or R for data analysis, including libraries such as Pandas, NumPy, Scikit\-learn, Matplotlib/Seaborn.
* Knowledge of statistical and Machine Learning models (e.g., regression, classification, clustering, time series) to solve business problems.
* Experience analyzing digital media consumption metrics, subscription models (SVOD), or advertising models (AVOD) is a plus.
* Strategic and Analytical Thinking: The ability to go beyond surface\-level data, connect the dots, and generate insights that drive business decisions.
* Detail\-Oriented with Analytical Rigor: Precision and accuracy in handling data, ensuring the validity and reliability of results.


**Additional Information:**
 At Disney, we believe it takes great people to create the memorable experiences, products and services our consumers know and love. We are a team of storytellers and hire under equal opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, disability and gender identity. We foster a corporate culture of belonging and inclusiveness, which empowers our staff and company to thrive. At Disney, everyone has the opportunity to speak up and participate, grow and share ideas.
   

  

*“You can design and create, and build the most wonderful place in the world. But it takes people to make the dream a reality.”* 
*Walt Disney*

title: Product Operations Manager, Banking & Capital Markets
description:
**About Us**
 Chainlink Labs is one of the primary contributing developers of Chainlink, the industry\-standard oracle platform bringing the capital markets onchain and powering the majority of decentralized finance. The Chainlink stack provides the essential data, interoperability, compliance, and privacy standards needed to power advanced blockchain use cases for institutional tokenized assets, Decentralized Finance (DeFi), payments, stablecoins, and more. Many of the world’s largest financial services institutions have also adopted Chainlink’s standards and infrastructure, including Swift, Euroclear, Mastercard, Fidelity International, UBS, ANZ, Aave, GMX, Lido, and many others.
   

  

 Chainlink Labs is a world\-class team of over 600 developers, researchers, and capital markets experts, and has ranked among Fortune's Best Workplaces in Technology, Fortune's Best Medium Workplace, and the Top 100 Global Most Loved Workplaces. Learn more at chain.link or chainlinklabs.com.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a Product Operations Manager, you will tackle a variety of strategic, analytical, and operational projects to drive growth for our business. This role will collaborate closely with the Product Organization to support product strategy, planning and product success. You will also work with executive leadership on critical projects, and require making strategic recommendations cross\-functionally.
   

  

**Your Impact**
* Collaborate directly with leaders across the organization on operations, strategy, product, and expansion efforts
* Contribute to high\-impact analytical, strategic, and operational initiatives
* Structure complex and ambiguous strategic problems
* Identify issues, develop hypotheses and execute in\-depth analyses to translate data into meaningful insights and make recommendations to the leadership team based on your findings
* Develop effective and scalable operational processes and fill operational gaps


**Qualifications**
* A Bachelor’s degree and 3\+ years of experience in product operations at a high\-growth tech company
* 2\+ years of experience working within the fintech space with a deep understanding of the capital markets industry.
* Passion, interest or deep curiosity for blockchain.
* You are highly analytical and data\-driven \-\- you can execute on complex analyses, define critical metrics, size opportunity areas, and measure and report on performance across the business
* You can think across multiple dimensions of a business (sales, product, marketing etc.) and work cross\-functionally to deliver results
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills
* Ability to flex between analytical, strategic and operational aspects of the job
* Resourceful and happy to roll up your sleeves to get any job done
* You want to work in a fast\-paced environment with constant change


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Experience having contributed to a blockchain project
* Experience working with Go\-To\-Market orgs
* Knowledge of SQL


*All roles with Chainlink Labs are global and remote\-based. Unless otherwise stated, we ask that you try to overlap some working hours with Eastern Standard Time (EST).*
*We carefully review all applications and aim to provide a response to every candidate within two weeks after the job posting closes.
 **The closing date is listed on the job advert, so we encourage you to take the time to thoughtfully prepare your application.** 
 We want to fully consider your experience and skills, and you will hear from us regarding the status of your application shortly after the closing date.*
**Commitment to Equal Opportunity**
 Chainlink Labs is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive equal consideration for employment in compliance with applicable laws, regulations, or ordinances. If you need assistance or accommodation due to a disability or special need when applying for a role or in our recruitment process, please contact us via this form.
   

  

**Global Data Privacy Notice for Job Candidates and Applicants**
 Information collected and processed as part of your Chainlink Labs Careers profile, and any job applications you choose to submit is subject to our Privacy Policy. By submitting your application, you are agreeing to our use and processing of your data as required.

title: Analista de Cuentas a Cobrar
description:
Importante empresa multinacional de industria oil \& gas, se encuentra en la búsqueda de un Analista de Cuentas a Cobrar cuyo propósito será controlar la facturación, realizar refacturaciones y efectuar el seguimiento de las cuentas corrientes de clientes. Pronosticar cobranzas mensuales y monitorear su evolución durante el mes.
 



**Principales responsabilidades:**



* Emitir re facturaciones de todas las sucursales en base a solicitudes especificas. Presentarlas a los clientes y monitorear su correspondiente cobranza.
* Realizar el seguimiento de las cuentas corrientes: contabilización de pagos de clientes, generando recibos y NC/ND por diferencia de cambio, en caso de corresponder, manteniendo las mismas actualizadas.
* Gestionar la documentación relacionada a Seguros necesaria para la habilitación de los recursos (vehículos y personal) con el fin de asegurar el ingreso del personal en los yacimientos para operar los equipos.
* Gestionar el patentamiento y transferencias en caso de altas o bajas de vehículos
* Asistir en todo lo que respecta a la gestión administrativa a la sucursal de BAS (atención telefónica, compras locales de consumibles), con el objetivo de asegurar el funcionamiento y soporte de la base.
* Identificación de cobranzas \+ retenciones impositivas para aplicación a las cuentas corrientes por cliente
* Cálculo y detalle de diferencias de cambio.
* Realización y envío a clientes de notas de débito por diferencia de cambio.
* Control de certificados WHC a través del portal SAP.
* Soporte a Operaciones en el seguimiento de documentos que habilitan a presentar facturas a clientes de CSW
* Presentación de facturas en portales de clientes CSW
* Gestión de cobranzas de clientes de CSW
* Reclamos de pagos a clientes via mail o en portales
* Determinación de la cobranza estimada mensual



**Requisitos:**



* Estudios terciarios o universitarios en relación al área (deseable)
* Preferentemente con experiencia en la industria oil\&gas
* Experiencia previa en mismas tareas
* Perfil es de una persona analítico y metódico
* Con experiencia idealmente en seguimiento de certificaciones de servicios
* Conocimientos de documentos y circuitos administrativos
* Conocimientos de conceptos contables.
* Buenas habilidades de comunicación y relaciones interpersonales.
* Manejo de Excel
* Deseable manejo de inglés intermedio
* Manejo de SAP (deseable).



**Horario:**



* Lunes a viernes de 9 a 18 hs (modalidad híbrida 3 x 2\)



  





**Beneficios:**



* Prepaga Osde
* Sistema de viandas
* Bono anual
* Clases de idioma



 A los interesados enviar cv sin omitir expectativa salarial.
 



 Gracias.

title: Data & Applied Intelligence
description:
**Data Scientist**
**🚀** 
**¿Qué desafíos te esperan?**
 Un Business Data Scientist es un perfil que se soporta sobre tres componentes fundamentales que son: sólidas habilidades de programación, fundamentos de estadística y análisis de datos y empatía con el negocio. Estos componentes, en conjunto con pensamiento analítico y capacidad de resolución de problemas permiten atacar problemas del mundo real apalancándose en datos. Un business data scientist es alguien que tiene un gran interés por resolver problemáticas de negocio con distintas técnicas existentes del mundo de los datos, eligiendo la más acorde según la problemática y la solución que mejor resuelve el punto de dolor del cliente y el usuario final.
   

  

 El Business Data Scientist va a ser protagonista de todo el proceso de una solución de Data Science, la cual puede ser implementada en una gran variedad de funciones o industrias: recolección y limpieza de datos; entrenamiento, tuneo y validación de modelos, así como también de la comunicación de los resultados a distintos grupos de stakeholders.
   

  

**Funciones del rol**
**🚀** 
**¿Qué tipo de perfil nos imaginamos?**
 A continuación, se presentan algunas de las funciones que un Business Data Scientist del equipo se puede encontrar desarrollando en su día a día como parte del staff de un proyecto:
   

  

  

- Colaborar al entendimiento de los requerimientos de negocio del cliente, y ayudar a traducir los mismos a acciones relacionadas al análisis de datos

- Realizar análisis exploratorios de conjuntos de datos para entender su comportamiento y extraer “insights”.

- Manipular conjuntos de datos para realizar operaciones de limpieza, transformación e integración sobre estos, de manera que puedan utilizarse como insumo de entrada para la obtención de modelos de ML u otro tipo de análisis.

- Desarrollar queries en SQL para extraer o analizar datos almacenados en fuentes relacionales.

- Utilizar técnicas/algoritmos de ML para la obtención y experimentación de modelos predictivos orientados a generar valor en los procesos del cliente

- Presentar resultados tanto a nivel interno como a clientes.

**Must Have**
 Los skills listados en esta sección constituyen requerimientos que un perfil de Business Data Scientist debe poseer
 **necesariamente** 
 para poder desenvolverse de forma adecuada dentro del equipo y los proyectos en general:
   

  

  

- Sólidas habilidades de programación en lenguajes orientados a Data Science, principalmente Python. Experiencia en otros lenguajes tales como R, Scala, Java, etc, también es valorada. (Excluyente)

- Fundamentos de estadística descriptiva e inferencial.

- Dominio básico de SQL y su aplicación en alguno de los motores comunes tales como PostgreSQL, MySQL, MS SQL Server, etc. (Excluyente)

- Dominio del stack para Data Science de Python, el cual se compone de algunas de las librerías comunes tales como Numpy, Pandas, Scipy, entre otras, para el análisis y limpieza de datos.

- Conocimientos sobre ML (aprendizaje supervisado y no supervisado)

- Inglés nivel Professional..

- Capacidad de presentación de resultados.

**Nice To Have**
 Habilidades de comunicación asertiva y efectiva ante distintos tipos de audiencias.
   

  

  

- Conocimiento sobre deep learning, LLMs y el proceso de fine\-tuning de los mismos.

- Dominio nivel avanzado de SQL para desarrollo de queries complejas.

- Experiencia desarrollando tableros y dashboards en herramientas como Tableau o PowerBI.

**Ok… si llegaste a leer hasta acá, te adelantamos lo que también es necesario que sepas:

title: Analista de Desarrollo BackEnd Fintech
description:
En Claro promovemos una cultura de respeto e igualdad de oportunidades. Seleccionamos a nuestros talentos basándonos en el valor que pueden agregar a nuestro negocio.
   

  

 Buscamos profesionales con capacidad para desenvolverse en un contexto de evolución e innovación digital, que generen nuevos desafíos y desarrollen oportunidades de negocios.
   

  

**Tu rol será:**
 Ser parte de un equipo de desarrollo ágil que tiene como objetivo implementación y diseño de la billetera de Claro "Claro Pay" en función de los requerimientos refinados por el equipo de análisis funcional que cumplan las necesidades del negocio.
   

  

**Tus principales desafíos serán:**
* Participar activamente en el equipo, diseñando y proponiendo alternativas superadoras que tengan un impacto real en la vida de los usuarios.
* Colaborar en definiciones de arquitectura, pensando siempre en soluciones escalables y asegurando la excelencia en la performance, permitiendo tener un código simple fácil de mantener aplicando tecnologías de vanguardia.
* Implementar patrones y buenas prácticas garantizando que funcione correctamente antes del pasaje a producción.
* Contribuir al mantenimiento evolutivo de un producto de alta calidad que se utiliza por millones de personas, pensando en disponibilidad y performance.


**Qué necesitamos de vos:**
* Profesional en Carrera relacionada a Sistemas o estudiante avanzando.
* Experiencia comprobable de 3 años en un puesto similar.
* Contar con conocimiento en Metodologias: Suite Attlasian (Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket), Metodologías Agiles, GIT / Jenkins, Equipos Devops – CI/CD
* Contar con conocimiento de tecnologías y plataformas: Java 21 (Programación Funcional, Streams), Spring Boot, Arquitectura de microservicios (sync/async) en HTTP, REST y SOAP, Patrones de diseño (SAGA, Circuit Breaker, DAO, Factory, Builder), Bases de datos relacionales (dominio de SQL).
* Contar con conocimiento de aplicaciones de alto rendimiento, escalabilidad y disponibilidad.
* Deseable que posea conocimientos de ElasticSearch y Kibana, OpenShift Concimiento en aplicaciones desplegadas en contenedores (Kubernetes Docker, OpenShift).
* Simplicidad en tu forma de pensar y hacer.
* Vocación de servicio.

title: Legal eDiscovery associate
description:
Ryz Labs is seeking an
 **experienced eDiscovery associate** 
 to support one of our U.S.\-based clients in managing end\-to\-end legal discovery workflows. This role will be key in strengthening their document response capabilities and supporting both large litigation cases and smaller investigations.
   

  

 What You’ll Do
   

  

* Run complete eDiscovery workflows: case setup, custodian management, legal holds, data collection, culling, review preparation, and export
* Maintain chain of custody, documentation rigor, and auditability across all cases
* Collaborate closely with internal legal teams and outside counsel
* Execute document search strategies and filtering using advanced keywords and metadata
* Support ongoing improvements in internal discovery and data management processes


 What We’re Looking For
   

  

* Hands\-on experience with eDiscovery tools (Purview, Reveal, or Onna preferred; other tools welcome)
* Familiarity with email systems, file storage, and cloud collaboration tools (Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, Slack, Teams)
* Solid understanding of legal discovery processes and terminology
* Proven experience working with legal teams or external counsel
* Excellent communication, attention to detail, and confidentiality standards


 Our values and what to expect:
   

  

* Customer First Mentality \- every decision we make should be made through the lens of the customer
* Bias for Action \- urgency is critical, expect that the timeline to get something done is accelerated
* Ownership \- step up if you see an opportunity to help, even if it's not your core responsibility
* Humility and Respect \- be willing to learn, be vulnerable, and treat everyone who interacts with RYZ with respect
* Frugality \- being frugal and cost\-conscious helps us do more with less
* Deliver Impact \- get things done most efficiently
* Raise our Standards \- always be looking to improve our processes, our team, our expectations. The status quo is not good enough and should never be

title: Medical Reviewer

 Make the right call and take your career to a whole new level. Join the company that focuses on its people and invests in their professional development and success.

title: Pharmacovigilance Analyst
description:
**Job Summary**
 Join our dynamic team as a Pharmacovigilance Specialist where you will play a crucial role in ensuring the safety and efficacy of pharmaceutical products. With a focus on Pharma Research \& Development you will contribute to the monitoring and evaluation of drug safety data. This hybrid role offers a balanced work environment with no travel required allowing you to make a significant impact from the comfort of your preferred location.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Monitor and evaluate adverse event reports to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.
* Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to analyze and interpret safety data.
* Assist in the preparation of safety reports and regulatory submissions.
* Support the development and maintenance of pharmacovigilance systems and processes.
* Conduct literature reviews to identify potential safety signals.
* Provide input into risk management plans and safety assessments.
* Ensure timely and accurate case processing and data entry.
* Participate in safety review meetings and contribute to decision\-making processes.
* Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge of pharmacovigilance regulations and guidelines.
* Assist in the training and mentoring of junior team members.
* Contribute to the continuous improvement of pharmacovigilance practices.
* Engage with stakeholders to communicate safety findings and recommendations.
* Utilize technical skills in Pharma Research \& Development to enhance data analysis capabilities.


**Qualifications**
* Possess a degree in a relevant scientific discipline.
* Demonstrate experience in Pharma Research \& Development.
* Exhibit strong analytical and problem\-solving skills.
* Show proficiency in pharmacovigilance data management.
* Have experience in PV Case Processing as a nice to have skill.
* Display excellent communication and teamwork abilities.
* Maintain a keen attention to detail and accuracy.


**Certifications Required**
 Certification in Pharmacovigilance or Drug Safety Management.

title: Project Director
description:
**Building on our past. Ready for the future**
 Worley is a global professional services company of energy, chemicals and resources experts headquartered in Australia.
   

  

 Right now, we’re bridging two worlds as we accelerate to more sustainable energy sources, while helping our customers provide the energy, chemicals and resources that society needs now.
   

  

 We partner with our customers to deliver projects and create value over the life of their portfolio of assets. We solve complex problems by finding integrated data\-centric solutions from the first stages of consulting and engineering to installation and commissioning, to the last stages of decommissioning and remediation. Join us and help drive innovation and sustainability in our projects.
   

  

**🎯** 

**Purpose**
* The Project Director shall head the project team through FEED, detailed design, procurement, construction, commissioning, start\-up and eventual handover of the plant and its associated facilities to the Employer.
* The Project Director is responsible and accountable for providing and managing an effective service for the Project and acquire oversight of the activities of EPC Contractor project management team.
* The Project Director will also ensure that pertinent information from the management team is promptly and clearly disseminated to EPC Contractor.
* The Project Director has the authority and responsibility for execution of the project in order to produce all work safely, with quality and within budget and schedule in compliance with contractual terms and conditions, as well as company policies.
* To ensure clear agreement of client and contractual objectives and that these are met or exceeded.
* To achieve or better the budgeted gross margin.
* To provide strong HSE leadership
* Direct responsibility and accountability for cost, schedule, quality, scope, safety and environment
* Direct responsibility and accountability for change management.


**📋** 

**Responsibilities**
* Direct the Integration of a Large Project Direct the implementation of all functions of project management. Direct the internal company environment to meet the needs of a large project. Direct the project through its development stages. Management of project initiation and team mobilization/start\-up and defining project team structure. Assigning tasks to project management team members and assessment performance and appraisals Liaising with the Employer. Obtaining Employer approvals as required. Monitoring, reporting and controlling project costs and schedule through all phases of the Project. Reporting of the project to stakeholders, contractors and Employer management. Managing the work scope, and the change management process. Developing and issuing the Project Execution Plan. Ensuring cost, schedule and performance metrics are rigorously managed. Administering project in accordance with contractual requirements and administration procedures. Management of Project risk. Ensuring technical integrity in all aspects and phases of the project. Ensuring technical assurance activities, such as design reviews and peer reviews are undertaken. \- Implementation of, and compliance with, the project Quality Program and Quality Plan. Leading Employer’s internal post\-completion audit of the project.
* HSE Management:


 Work with project manager(s) to ensure that HSE requirements are documented and understood by all project participants
   

  

 Compliance with HSE procedures
   

  

 Maintain HSE focus for project duration
   

  

 Ensuring compliance with Employer requirements as well as statuary and regulatory requirements.
   

  

 Leading the EHS program. \- Managing interfaces between various stakeholders and contractors. \- Identification and recommendation of resource requirements.
   

  

 Performance evaluation of relevant personnel.
   

  

 Leading project to safe and successful pre\-commissioning, commissioning, start up and handover. \- Management of project handover, lessons learned and project closeout.
   

  

* Client Relationships:


 Identify, document and communicate the client requirements
   

  

 Effective management of multiple stakeholders (internal and external)
   

  

 Maintain and build healthy client relationships throughout the project and post completion.
   

  

 Proactively resolve performance, scope, cost and schedule issues with the Client as they arise, in conjunction with the Project Manager(s).
   

  

* Commercial Management: Effective management of Worley/Client contract, including delivery of the budget gross margin.
* Scope Management:


 Effective handover from Business Development Group
   

  

 Clear definition of scope
   

  

 Compliance with requirements of the contract
   

  

 Effective change control process application
   

  

 Manage and monitor project performance on an ongoing basis to identify issues and opportunities early and take pre\-emptive action.
   

  

 Effectively manages project interfaces, in conjunction with the Project Manager(s), including the use of High Value Engineering centres.
   

  

* Time Management: Direct the planning of all scope elements of the project, with the support of the Project Manager(s). Develop resource \& financial requirements from the project plan. Direct the management of the design, procurement and construction activities to meet the project schedule Ownership of the project schedule Routine monitoring of the key reports and development of corrective action plans. Timely and effective documentation turnover and project close\-out.
* Cost Control: Establish cost report for control of projects. Monitoring and control of all cost aspects of the project to forecast costs, obtain approvals, identify trends and take action to avoid cost overruns and take advantage of opportunities Assess performance of cost control system \& personnel.
* Quality Management: ght sys


 Define the project quality requirements.
   

  

 Ensure the ritems are in place and that these are applied on the project.
   

  

 Development of a project schedule which allows work to be executed in a logical sequence.
   

  

 Compliance with Worley policies and procedures.
   

  

 Implement lessons learned system
   

  

**🔍 Requirements**
**What you will bring**
* Established/Proven Leadership record during FEED, Detailed Design and Construction project phases.
* Minimum of 15\-20 years industry experience in Project Management role.
* Previous experience in managing major projects(\+500M$USD).
* Strong Contractual acumen
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
* Proven ability to lead multidiscipline team; Ability to direct a project, with the direct supervision and coordination provided by the Project Manager(s).
* Demonstrable commitment to HSE Management in a project environment
* Excellent leadership and communication skills
* High level of customer focus


 If you need
 **any accessibility adjustments** 
 , please make sure to mention it in your application
   

  

**Moving forward together**
 We want our people to be energized and empowered to drive sustainable impact. So, our focus is on a values\-inspired culture that unlocks brilliance through belonging, connection and innovation.
   

  

 We’re building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace. Creating a space where everyone feels they belong, can be themselves, and are heard.
   

  

 And we're not just talking about it; we're doing it. We're reskilling our people, leveraging transferable skills, and supporting the transition of our workforce to become experts in today's low carbon energy infrastructure and technology.
   

  

 Whatever your ambition, there’s a path for you here. And there’s no barrier to your potential career success. Join us to broaden your horizons, explore diverse opportunities, and be part of delivering sustainable change

title: Sales Professional
description:
**A Snapshot of Your Day**
 As a Regional Sales Manager (f/m/d) you will work on interesting and meaningful opportunities in a highly motivated, team. You will be leading sales activities in the allocated region.
   

  

**How You’ll Make An Impact**
* Selling and account management activities
* Face\-to\-face sales to new customers and cross/up/repeat sales to existing customers
* Assessing customer needs and suggesting appropriate products, services, and/or solutions
* Developing and delivering sales bids/proposals/presentations and conducting product demonstrations
* Developing medium to long\-term sales plans and preparing strategies to protect, grow, and diversify the relationship with targeted customers
* Selling directly to the ultimate purchaser or indirectly through channel partners.


**What You Bring**
* A sound Degree in Mechanical Engineering or equivalent experience
* Extensive Sales experience (\+8 years preferred) with good knowledge in Siemens Energy product and specially in Turbo machineries.

* Ability to respond to common inquiries or complaints from customers, regulatory agencies, or members of the business community. Ability to effectively present information to higher management, public groups, Executive staff, the Management Committee and key client groups.
* Proficiency in English and Spanish is necessary; fluency in Portuguese also will be good.
* Ability to travel mainly within Latin Americas and some intercontinental travel for up to or exceeding 40%,


**About The Team**
 Our
 **Transformation of Industry** 
 division is decarbonizing the industrial sector. Increasing electrification and efficiency are key and demand for green H2 and derivative fuels will rise. We enable decarbonization of the industrial sector and the transition to sustainable processes, building on a strong industrial customer base, a global network, diverse technologies, and integrated execution capabilities. Candidates want to learn about the divisions they will be joining–the structure, how it works together, and the role it plays in driving Siemens Energy’s mission forward.
   

  

**Who is Siemens Energy?**
 At Siemens Energy, we are more than just an energy technology company. With \~100,000 dedicated employees in more than 90 countries, we develop the energy systems of the future, ensuring that the growing energy demand of the global community is met reliably and sustainably. The technologies created in our research departments and factories drive the energy transition and provide the base for one sixth of the world's electricity generation.
   

  

 Our global team is committed to making sustainable, reliable, and affordable energy a reality by pushing the boundaries of what is possible. We uphold a 150\-year legacy of innovation that encourages our search for people who will support our focus on decarbonization, new technologies, and energy transformation.
   

  

 Find out how you can make a difference at Siemens Energy: https://www.siemens\-energy.com/employeevideo
   

  

**Our Commitment to Diversity**
 Lucky for us, we are not all the same. Through diversity, we generate power. We run on inclusion and our combined creative energy is fueled by over 130 nationalities. Siemens Energy celebrates character – no matter what ethnic background, gender, age, religion, identity, or disability. We energize society, all of society, and we do not discriminate based on our differences.
   

  

**Rewards/Benefits**
* Work environment that promotes diversity, equality, and individuality.
* Training and learning opportunities on our e\-learning platform.
* Hybrid and flexible work schedule (except for technical positions on site).
* Extended shared parental leave.
* Competitive compensation commensurate with responsibilities.

description:
Puesto: Diseñador Comercial – Área de Luminarias
   

  

 Descripción general:
   

 El Diseñador Comercial del área de luminarias es responsable de integrar el diseño, la técnica y la estrategia comercial en el desarrollo de proyectos de iluminación para clientes residenciales, corporativos y arquitectónicos. Su objetivo es transformar las necesidades del cliente en soluciones de diseño funcionales y estéticas, alineadas con la identidad de Rodina y las oportunidades comerciales del área.
   

  

 ⸻
   

  

 Responsabilidades principales:
   

 • Diseñar y desarrollar propuestas de luminarias a medida, tanto para proyectos de arquitectura e interiorismo como para colecciones propias.

 • Participar en reuniones con clientes, arquitectos e interioristas para interpretar requerimientos y ofrecer soluciones técnicas y estéticas.

 • Coordinar con el equipo de producción y oficina técnica la viabilidad técnica y el seguimiento de cada proyecto.
   

 • Participar en la definición de nuevos productos y colecciones, aportando visión de diseño y análisis de tendencias.
   

 • Supervisar la correcta implementación de los diseños en fábrica y en las instalaciones finales.
   

 • Asegurar que cada proyecto refleje los estándares de calidad, diseño y terminación propios de la marca Rodina.
   

  

 ⸻
   

  

 Perfil buscado:
   

 • Diseñador Industrial, de Interiores o afín, con sólida sensibilidad estética y técnica.
   

 • Experiencia en diseño de luminarias, mobiliario o equipamiento arquitectónico (mínimo 2 años).
   

 • Perfil comercial y orientado a resultados.
   

 • Conocimiento de materiales, procesos productivos y sistemas de iluminación.
   

 • Capacidad para presentar y comunicar ideas de diseño a clientes.
   

 • Manejo de herramientas como AutoCAD, SketchUp, Rhino o similares, y software de renderizado.
   

 • Actitud proactiva, espíritu colaborativo y orientación a la excelencia.
   

  

 ⸻
   

  

 Modalidad de trabajo:
   

  

 Presencial.
   

 Ubicación: Fábrica Rodina Design.
   

 Reporta a: Dirección de Diseño y Desarrollo / Gerencia Comercial.

title: Director Application Support, Estimation
description:
**Description**
****Director, Software Engineering****
 Hyperscalers (Enterprise Sizing \& Estimation)
   

  

 ARGENTINA \- LATAM
   

  

 HYBRID
   

  

****Job Details****
 Salesforce \- Corporate Strategy \- Hyperscalers is seeking a visionary Director of Software Engineering to lead a high\-impact engineering organization within the Corporate Strategy team. This role will lead the development of Salesforce’s Estimation\-as\-a\-Service (EaaS) platform — the core technology enabling our transition to a scalable, consumption\-based business model.
   

  

 The ideal candidate is a hands\-on technology leader who can connect architectural vision with business outcomes, build and mentor diverse technical teams, and deliver reliable, high\-performing software systems that power Salesforce’s growth.
   

  

 You will partner closely with Product, Architecture, and Analytics leaders to define and execute the technical strategy that supports Estimation Excellence across internal and external channels — from our APIs to the customer experience layer.
   

  

 If you are passionate about platform\-level architecture, leading through influence, and empowering teams to deliver measurable business impact, this is your opportunity to shape Salesforce’s next decade of growth.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Lead, mentor, and scale multiple engineering teams focused on building core estimation services and APIs under the Hyperscalers initiative.
* Define and execute a technical vision aligned with Salesforce’s North Star Architecture, emphasizing modular, API\-driven, and scalable design principles.
* Collaborate with product, UX, and data leaders to deliver cohesive solutions that enable faster, more accurate, and data\-driven estimation.
* Oversee the design, implementation, and operation of systems supporting estimation, pricing, and value (ROI) logic for consumption\-based products.
* Ensure architectural consistency, reliability, and observability across all services.
* Partner with cross\-functional leaders to align engineering priorities with revenue, platform, and customer success goals.
* Champion a culture of innovation, ownership, and technical excellence — encouraging experimentation, learning, and accountability.
* Drive hiring, career development, and succession planning within the engineering organization.
* Promote diversity of thought and inclusion as catalysts for innovation and better business outcomes


****Key Outcomes****
* A cohesive, scalable engineering organization with clear ownership of estimation domains (size, price, value).
* Standardized, well\-documented APIs powering estimation capabilities across multiple Salesforce products and channels.
* High\-performing teams delivering measurable impact on deal velocity, quoting accuracy, and customer trust.
* Seamless collaboration across Engineering, Product, Architecture, and Analytics.
* Strong engineering culture rooted in clarity, autonomy, and continuous improvement.


**Qualifications**
* Proven experience leading and growing high\-performing software engineering teams in complex, distributed systems environments.
* Strong architectural background, with experience designing API\-first, service\-oriented, or microservice\-based systems at enterprise scale.
* Demonstrated ability to connect technology strategy to business value and measurable outcomes.
* Deep understanding of modern development practices: DevOps, CI/CD, observability, and agile delivery.
* Excellent leadership and communication skills — able to inspire technical teams and influence cross\-functional partners.
* Experience in SaaS or consumption\-based platforms preferred.
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities; comfortable navigating ambiguity.
* Commitment to diversity, inclusion, and team development.

title: Consult Partner
description:
**Who We Are**
 At Kyndryl, we design, build, manage and modernize the mission\-critical technology systems that the world depends on every day. So why work at Kyndryl? We are always moving forward – always pushing ourselves to go further in our efforts to build a more equitable, inclusive world for our employees, our customers and our communities.
   

  

**The Role**
 Kyndryl Consult is the fastest growing business within the organization and instrumental to the company’s strategic growth objectives. You will play a key leadership role across multi\-disciplinary teams, guiding them through complex consulting engagements and be responsible for creating and positioning strategic change agendas within Kyndryl's largest accounts across the C\-suite, spanning the intersection between complex Business and IT customer solutions, transformations that impact across the entire customer Eco\-system.
   

  

 As a Consult Partner, you will have a pivotal role in driving profitable growth, leveraging our comprehensive capabilities to build strong client relationships, and leading your team to success. This role demands a strong presence, industry expertise, and the ability to cultivate relationships with CXO\-level clients. Your leadership will be critical in fostering talent development and innovation within the organization.
   

  

 This role will also be leading the overall client engagement and delivering high quality client work alongside senior client stakeholders. Working in a transaction environment which may be pre or post deal, planning post deal implementation, and ultimately supporting execution. Partners will be focused on financial impact, utilization and contribution, client impact and leadership.
   

  

 Contribute to Profitable Growth:
   

  

* Drive significant financial outcomes through signings and revenue targets
* Ensure sustained growth and profitability, managing margin expectations and backlog growth
* Support the identification, pursuit and conversion of a pipeline of business development opportunities
* Undertake scoping and fee negotiation on engagements, while enduring profitability and understanding and containing risk


 Client Engagement:
   

  

* Build and maintain deep relationships with client CXOs and be seen as the 'go to' person and a trusted advisor by senior executives
* Leading C\-level client interactions and consulting initiatives, deliverables and outputs of a deal engagement
* Demonstrate credibility and experience to advise and deliver on complex consulting engagement
* Enhance client satisfaction, as measured by Net Promoter Score (NPS) and new relationship development; Secure client references


 Operational Excellence:
   

  

* Achieve individual and team utilization targets
* Lead the design of complex engagements and take responsibility for oversight of delivery, ensuring high performance and customer satisfaction


 Leadership, Management, People
   

  

* Lead by example; Fostering a culture of continuous personal and professional development and challenging our people to be curious and innovative and supportive for each other. At the same time ensuring that all outcomes are commercially focused, value adding and effectively executed


 Strategic Contribution:
   

  

* Utilize industry and technology expertise to shape and drive the company’s strategic initiatives.
* Align with Kyndryl’s strategic vision and contribute to its execution.
* Drive external eminence and innovation, establishing a strong personal and organizational brand in the industry.
* Proactively develop thought leadership and intellectual capital


**Who You Are**
 Who You Are
   

  

 You’re good at what you do and possess the required experience to prove it. However, equally as important – you have a growth mindset; keen to drive your own personal and professional development. You are customer\-focused – someone who prioritizes customer success in their work. And finally, you’re open and borderless – naturally inclusive in how you work with others.
   

  

**Required Skills And Experience**
* Extensive experience in client engagement and relationship management at the CXO level
* Expertise in the mining and energy sectors or banking and financial services industry
* Demonstrable ability to build and commercialize relationships with senior executives
* Proven track record of leading and executing complex projects with multi\-disciplinary teams in a consulting, corporate or public sector environment
* Effective financial acumen with experience in driving revenue growth and managing margins
* Experience of managing or supporting high\-value business development activities with senior stakeholders
* Deep understanding of industry trends and technology
* Sound personal brand and presence in the industry
* Demonstrated ability to innovate and drive change


**Being You**
 Diversity is a whole lot more than what we look like or where we come from, it’s how we think and who we are. We welcome people of all cultures, backgrounds, and experiences. But we’re not doing it single\-handily: Our Kyndryl Inclusion Networks are only one of many ways we create a workplace where all Kyndryls can find and provide support and advice. This dedication to welcoming everyone into our company means that Kyndryl gives you – and everyone next to you – the ability to bring your whole self to work, individually and collectively, and support the activation of our equitable culture. That’s the Kyndryl Way.
   

  

**What You Can Expect**
 With state\-of\-the\-art resources and Fortune 100 clients, every day is an opportunity to innovate, build new capabilities, new relationships, new processes, and new value. Kyndryl cares about your well\-being and prides itself on offering benefits that give you choice, reflect the diversity of our employees and support you and your family through the moments that matter – wherever you are in your life journey. Our employee learning programs give you access to the best learning in the industry to receive certifications, including Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Skillsoft, and many more. Through our company\-wide volunteering and giving platform, you can donate, start fundraisers, volunteer, and search over 2 million non\-profit organizations. At Kyndryl, we invest heavily in you, we want you to succeed so that together, we will all succeed.
   

  

**Get Referred!**
 If you know someone that works at Kyndryl, when asked ‘How Did You Hear About Us’ during the application process, select ‘Employee Referral’ and enter your contact's Kyndryl email address.

title: Semi Senior Operations Coordinator
description:
Please note that we will never request payment or bank account information at any stage of the recruitment process. As we continue to grow our teams, we urge you to be cautious of fraudulent job postings or recruitment activities that misuse our company name and information. Please protect your personal information during any recruitment process. While Monks may contact potential candidates via LinkedIn, all applications must be submitted through our official website (monks.com/careers).
   

  

 We are seeking an experienced and dynamic Operations Coordinator to join our team on a large\-scale, US based automotive client. The ideal candidate will have 2\-4 years of operations (or adjacent) experience within an advertising agency or a related field. This is an exciting opportunity for someone who wants to build processes from the ground up and contribute to a bespoke delivery center of excellence. This role requires someone with a passion for attention to detail and process documentation.
 **This is a hybrid role based in Buenos Aires Argentina**
**Key Responsibilities**
* Set up and maintain all internal tools and systems to ensure consistency and accuracy across the entire client portfolio
* Oversee the creation and organization for all projects, ensuring that all necessary documentation is complete and up\-to\-date
* Develop and implement standard operating procedures (SOPs) for internal workflows, ensuring consistency and efficiency across the client engagement
* Assist with financial aspects of projects, including time tracking and burn reporting
* Coordinate with department heads to allocate resources for special projects effectively
* Liaise with project management team to help manage retained team capacity
* Maintain and organize internal databases and project management tools to ensure all team members have access to current and accurate information.
* Ensure compliance with internal policies, industry standards, and regulatory requirements. Prepare for and support internal and external audits as needed.
* Assist with generation of any ad hoc reporting needs as it relates to the data stored in our internal systems and tools
* Collaborate with internal teams to streamline communication and enhance overall operational efficiency.
* Foster a culture of continuous learning and improvement


**Qualifications**
* Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Advertising, Business, or a related field.
* Previous agency experience strongly preferred
* 2\-4 years of operations or adjacent discipline (such as project management or client services) preferred.
* Strong understanding of advertising and marketing principles.
* Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills.
* Proficiency in project management software and tools (e.g., Monday.com and / or JIRA strongly preferred).
* Strong problem\-solving skills and the ability to make decisions under pressure.
* Detail\-oriented with exceptional organizational skills.
* Ability to adapt to a fast\-paced, dynamic work environment.


**About Monks**
 Monks is the global, purely digital, unitary operating brand of S4Capital plc. With a legacy of innovation and specialized expertise, Monks combines an extraordinary range of global marketing and technology services to accelerate business possibilities and redefine how brands and businesses interact with the world. Its integration of systems and workflows delivers unfettered content production, scaled experiences, enterprise\-grade technology and data science fueled by AI—managed by the industry’s best and most diverse digital talent—to help the world’s trailblazing companies outmaneuver and outpace their competition.
   

  

 Monks was named a Contender in The Forrester Wave™: Global Marketing Services. It has remained a constant presence on Adweek’s Fastest Growing lists (2019\-23\), ranks among Cannes Lions' Top 10 Creative Companies (2022\-23\) and is the only partner to have been placed in AdExchanger’s Programmatic Power Players list every year (2020\-24\). In addition to being named Adweek’s first AI Agency of the Year (2023\), Monks has been recognized by Business Intelligence in its 2024 Excellence in Artificial Intelligence Awards program in three categories: the Individual category, Organizational Winner in AI Strategic Planning and AI Product for its service Monks.Flow. Monks has also garnered the title of Webby Production Company of the Year (2021\-24\), won a record number of FWAs and has earned a spot on Newsweek’s Top 100 Global Most Loved Workplaces 2023\.
   

  

 We are an equal\-opportunity employer committed to building a respectful and empowering work environment for all people to freely express themselves amongst colleagues who embrace diversity in all respects. Including fresh voices and unique points of view in all aspects of our business not only creates an environment where we can all grow and thrive but also increases our potential to produce work that better represents—and resonates with—the world around us.

title: Head of AI Creative Studio
description:
**About CookUnity**
 Food has lost its soul to modern convenience. And with it, it has lost the power to nourish, inspire, and connect us. So in 2018, CookUnity was founded as the first\-of\-its\-kind platform that connects the world with the source of truly great food: chefs. Today, CookUnity delivers 50 million meals a year from the industry’s best chefs to homes all over the country. Fresh. Ready\-to\-eat. And crafted with the passion that nourishes body and soul.
   

  

 Unwilling to stop there, CookUnity is expanding beyond delivery to become an ever\-innovating marketplace focused on our singular mission:
 **empower Chefs to nourish the world.**
 If that mission has you hungry in more ways than one, you’ve found the right job posting.
   

  

**About The Creative Studio**
 The Creative Studio at CookUnity is where storytelling, innovation, and technology meet. From chef campaigns to video content and brand design, the team brings the CookUnity experience to life across every touchpoint.
   

  

 As AI reshapes how stories are made, we’re building an
 **AI Creative Studio** 
 to harness the power of generative technology — enabling faster content creation, personalized storytelling, and scalable brand impact while preserving the soul of our chef\-driven brand.
   

  

**The Role**
 The
 **Head of AI Creative Studio** 
 will establish and lead CookUnity’s AI\-driven creative function. Reporting to the
 **SVP of Growth** 
 , this role combines creativity, technology, and leadership to define how CookUnity scales storytelling and performance creative through generative AI.
   

  

 You’ll own the strategy, tooling, and creative systems that power everything from personalized campaign assets to AI\-assisted production workflows. This is a unique opportunity to shape the intersection of art and intelligence inside a fast\-growing brand.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Define the AI Creative Vision:


 Build and own the roadmap for CookUnity’s AI Creative Studio, aligning it with brand, growth, and content goals.
   

  

* Lead the AI Creative Function:


 Recruit, mentor, and manage a hybrid team of creative technologists, prompt engineers, designers, editors, and producers.
   

  

* Systemize AI Workflows:


 Develop prompt libraries, creative pipelines, governance frameworks, and brand guardrails for AI\-assisted content generation.
   

  

* Drive Experimentation \& Innovation:


 Pilot new tools and techniques (image, video, copy, personalization) to drive measurable improvements in content velocity and performance.
   

  

* Cross\-Functional Collaboration:


 Partner with Brand, Growth Marketing, CRM, Product, and Engineering teams to deploy AI\-powered creative in campaigns, onboarding flows, and personalization.
   

  

* Quality \& Brand Stewardship:


 Ensure all AI\-assisted output reflects CookUnity’s chef\-driven identity and maintains creative integrity.
   

  

* Measurement \& Optimization:


 Define success metrics and continuously optimize for creative efficiency, engagement, and conversion impact.
   

  

* AI Ethics \& Compliance:


 Establish and uphold ethical standards, content safety checks, and compliance frameworks across all AI\-driven production.
   

  

**Qualifications**
* 10\+ years of experience in creative, marketing, or design leadership roles with a track record of innovation.
* 3\+ years of hands\-on experience with generative AI or creative technology tools (e.g., Midjourney, Google Veo 3, Runway, OpenAI, Adobe Firefly, Freepik, ElevenLabs).
* Proven success building and leading hybrid creative and technical teams.
* Strong understanding of performance marketing, brand storytelling, and content operations.
* Exceptional communication, presentation, and organizational skills.
* Ability to translate complex technology into scalable creative systems.
* Entrepreneurial mindset with comfort in fast\-paced, evolving environments.
* Deep curiosity for AI, creativity, and human\-machine collaboration.


 Personal Characteristics
   

  

* Visionary yet pragmatic — able to think big while building scalable systems.
* Deeply collaborative with strong influence across functions.
* Passionate about creativity, culture, and emerging technologies.
* Hands\-on problem solver who balances data with intuition.
* Resilient, adaptive, and inspired by experimentation.


**Learn More About CookUnity**
 We believe great leadership starts with alignment on vision, values, and ways of working. To give you deeper insight into who we are and what we’re looking for, we invite you to explore:
 **CookUnity's Leadership Principles** 
 – The values and behaviors that guide how we operate, collaborate, and scale.
   

  

 We hope this provides valuable insight into our culture and product vision. If this excites you, we’d love to connect!
   

  

**Benefits**
 💸 Get paid in USD.
   

  

 🗺 Work remotely: design the life that you want
   

  

 ⛱ Enjoy 15 days of vacation each year from the start date
   

  

 🎄 16 fully paid Argentinean holidays
   

  

 🩺 Healthcare Benefit: Monthly stipend to use in your preferred healthcare provider
   

  

 🗓️ 5\- year Sabbatical: After 5 years with CookUnity, you get a 4\-week paid sabbatical
   

  

 🐣 Paid Family leave
   

  

 🕯 Compassionate Leave: 3\-5 days each time the need arises
   

  

 🧘🏽‍♀️ Customize the benefits that suit your needs! Access a range of perks tailored to you, including learning opportunities, wellness memberships, delivery apps, and more through our comprehensive benefit platform
   

  

 🧑‍🏫 Personalized English coach
   

  

 If you’re interested in this role, please submit your application and if we think you might be a fit, we'll get in touch with you. Thank you for your time!
   

  

*CookUnity is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are dedicated to creating a community of inclusion and an environment free from discrimination or harassment. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, citizenship status, protected veteran status, genetic information, or physical or mental disability.*

title: Staff Augmentation Talent Advisor
description:
Chevron’s Business Support Center (BASSC), located in Buenos Aires, is accepting applications for the position of
 **Staff Augmentation Talent Advisor** 
 . Successful candidates will join the IT Organization, which is part of a multifunction service center with a workforce of more than 1800 employees that deliver business services and solutions to the corporation across the globe.
   

  

 This role serves as a strategic partner and resource manager for external staff augmentation, ensuring effective labor resource delivery. In collaboration with the local Talent Site Lead, the Talent Advisor works closely with IT preferred suppliers and departments to place and retain top talent, deliver value, and align with strategic priorities.
   

  

 The Talent Advisor also plays a key role in developing local talent. This includes identifying skill gaps, designing targeted development initiatives to build a sustainable and skilled local workforce.
   

  

 Additionally, they leverage technology, data, and workforce planning tools to anticipate future demand. This includes understanding upcoming projects, identifying the types of solutions and skills that will be needed, and proactively addressing talent gaps before they impact delivery.
   

  

**Responsibilities for this position may include but are not limited to:**
* Optimize talent mix and geographic distribution to maintain cost efficiency while ensuring compliance with best practices.
* Foster seamless collaboration across departments and suppliers to enhance operational efficiency and business outcomes.
* Meet business needs with high\-quality resources through strategic partnerships that drive performance and value creation.
* Design and implement initiatives to develop local talent and build a sustainable, skilled workforce.
* Anticipate future talent needs and proactively address skill gaps through demand planning and technology\-enabled workflows.
* Advise on staff augmentation strategies aligned with enterprise priorities and guide leaders on contractor management.
* Manage relationships with key suppliers and oversee work orders, rate negotiations, and resource allocation.
* Educate stakeholders on contractor governance processes and ensure data quality related to staff augmentation.
* Collaborate with technical and regional leads to secure budget approvals and align sourcing strategies with geographic targets.
* Influence strategic sourcing decisions and support internal capability building through tailored training programs.
* Partner with educational institutions and community programs to strengthen the talent pipeline.
* Forecast emerging skill requirements and align resourcing strategies with long\-term business goals.


**Required Qualifications**
* Prior experience in IT talent acquisition or vendor management.
* Familiarity with governance processes and compliance standards.
* Exposure to regional delivery models and global talent strategies.
* Ability to manage contractor conversion and headcount planning in collaboration with site leads.


**Relocation Options:**
* Relocation will not be considered.


**International Considerations**
* Expatriate assignments will not be considered.


 Chevron regrets that it is unable to sponsor employment Visas or consider individuals on time\-limited Visa status for this position
 **.**

title: Especialista en Análisis de Riesgos
description:
**Nueva oportunidad en el CFI:** 
 Estamos buscando un/a
 **Especialista en Análisis de Riesgo Crediticio.** 
  
 



  






 En el
 
**Consejo Federal de Inversiones (CFI)** 

 queremos sumar a
 
**l Área de Financiamiento Productivo** 

 a una persona con experiencia y motivación para contribuir al análisis integral del riesgo crediticio de proyectos productivos y de inversión en todo el país. 
 




  






 El puesto tiene como propósito evaluar la capacidad de pago y el perfil financiero de personas físicas y jurídicas, elaborando recomendaciones fundadas que promuevan una administración prudente y sostenible del crédito.  
 




  





**Principales responsabilidades** 

  
 



* Analizar solicitudes de crédito de personas físicas y jurídicas, evaluando capacidad de pago, historial financiero, situación patrimonial y comportamiento crediticio.
* Relevar, verificar y validar documentación financiera y comercial (balances, declaraciones juradas, estados de cuenta, antecedentes comerciales y referencias bancarias).
* Elaborar informes de riesgo crediticio y emitir recomendaciones técnicas fundamentadas para la aprobación, rechazo o condicionamiento del crédito solicitado.
* Elaborar reportes sobre el estado de la cartera crediticia y monitorear su evolución.
* Aplicar modelos de scoring y herramientas de evaluación cuantitativa, considerando variables financieras y no financieras (sector, contexto económico, historial del cliente).
* Determinar condiciones de otorgamiento (monto, plazo, tasa, garantías) en función del perfil de riesgo del solicitante.
* Interactuar con las áreas involucradas en el proceso de otorgamiento y con las unidades de enlace y referentes provinciales.
* Contribuir a la actualización de matrices de riesgo, manuales y políticas de crédito, en línea con la normativa vigente y las mejores prácticas del sector.
* Colaborar con el área territorial en acciones de promoción y difusión.




  
 





  
 





  
 




**Requisitos excluyentes:** 

  
 



* Título universitario, terciario o tecnicaturas de duración no inferior a TRES (3\) años. Se valoran carreras en Ciencias Económicas o afines.
* Experiencia laboral mínima de seis (6\) meses en funciones vinculadas al análisis o gestión de crédito, preferentemente en organismos o entidades bancarias y financiera




  
 




**Requisitos deseables:** 

  
 



* Se valorará formación en Administración, Administración de Empresas o Contador/a Público/a.
* Dominio de herramientas informáticas avanzadas, especialmente Excel (modelos financieros), sistemas de gestión crediticia (CRM, scoring bancario) y herramientas de Business Intelligence (Power BI, Tableau, SQL)..
* Conocimientos en riesgo crediticio, metodologías de evaluación, scoring, políticas de crédito y herramientas de mitigación.
* Capacidad de análisis financiero avanzado, interpretación de estados contables, ratios y flujos de fondos.
* Conocimiento de normativas locales e internacionales vinculadas a crédito y gestión de riesgo (BCRA, CNV, UIF).
* Habilidad para la redacción de informes técnicos y financieros.
* Conocimiento del contexto sectorial y de las economías regionales, aportando una mirada cualitativa al análisis crediticio
 
 .




  
 




  





**Competencias y habilidades requeridas:** 

  
 



* **Pensamiento analítico y crítico:** 

 Capacidad para descomponer información compleja, identificar patrones y variables relevantes y elaborar juicios fundados que respalden decisiones de crédito. Implica interpretar datos cuantitativos y cualitativos de forma rigurosa, anticipar escenarios y evaluar el impacto de los riesgos potenciales.




  
 



* **Atención al detalle:** 

 Rigurosidad en el tratamiento de datos financieros y documentación de respaldo, asegurando consistencia y confiabilidad en los análisis. Supone validar la información de forma meticulosa y detectar inconsistencias que puedan afectar la evaluación crediticia.




  
 



* **Comunicación efectiva:** 

 Habilidad para transmitir resultados y recomendaciones técnicas de manera clara, estructurada y comprensible para distintos interlocutores (áreas comerciales, legales, territoriales y directivos). Implica redactar informes de riesgo con claridad y fundamento técnico.




  
 



* **Autonomía** 

 : Capacidad para ejecutar tareas con criterio técnico propio, priorizando la calidad del análisis y el cumplimiento de plazos. Implica actuar con independencia dentro del marco de lineamientos definidos por la jefatura del área y asumir la responsabilidad por los resultados de su labor.




  
 



* **Capacidad de negociación:** 

 Habilidad para coordinar con otras áreas del CFI y con referentes territoriales, buscando consensos y soluciones que equilibren prudencia crediticia y desarrollo regional. Supone defender criterios técnicos de manera argumentada, manteniendo relaciones colaborativas.




  
 



* **Trabajo en equipo:** 

 Predisposición para compartir información, aportar conocimiento técnico y cooperar con otros profesionales en la gestión integral del riesgo. Implica contribuir al logro de los objetivos del área y al fortalecimiento de la cultura organizacional del CFI.




  
 



* **Adaptabilidad y Aprendizaje:**
